pattern: Binary search on the answer combined with inclusion-exclusion over LCMs to count how many valid amounts are <= a given value.
hints: Binary search on the answer x: find the smallest x such that the count of positive multiples of any coin <= x is >= k.
Use inclusion-exclusion on the coin set: count multiples of each coin, subtract double-counted intersections (LCM of pairs), add back triple intersections, etc.
Since coins are at most 15 in number, enumerate all 2^15 non-empty subsets; for each subset compute the LCM and add or subtract floor(x / LCM) depending on subset size parity.

edge_inputs.description: single coin k=1 — answer is the coin itself
coins share factors — LCM deduplication matters
k larger than first few multiples
one coin divides another — subsets with larger coin add nothing

stress.description: random subset of distinct coins 1-25 with moderate k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    all_coins = list(range(1, 26))
    rng.shuffle(all_coins)
    coins = sorted(all_coins[:size])
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [coins, k]
